title: Authorization, Tenancy, Object, Function, and Property Policy · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken deny-by-default resource authorization decision; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deny-by-default resource authorization decision and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.
Load only enough identity or policy data to enforce ownership and object access without trusting a user-selected identifier.
Derive tenant from the principal, carry it through repository interfaces, constrain every query and cache key, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Authz Subject Resource Action
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 4840 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.

Inspect the Authz Subject Resource Action trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Authz Subject Resource Action.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects. For Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Authz Subject Resource Action, keep Authz Subject Resource Action connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Denial Disclosure Audit
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: httpsts-denial-disclosure-audit-1if9t7" that uses case 4035 to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or Express, open listeners, contact networks or databases, spawn workers, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24 and Express 5 transfer gates.

Competency probe: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Denial Disclosure Audit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Every authorization denial should explain the complete policy failure to the caller.
